finding: Group task with weaker member achieves longer persistence than solo effort, but verbal encouragement from stronger member reduces this gain, suggesting encouragement shifts perception of relative ability away from the motivating asymmetry.

Deleterious single-nucleotide polymorphisms cluster across three functional modules (immune, hormone, metabolic), with 517 variants in the top 10% deleteriousness threshold, suggesting multi-system genetic predisposition.

Priming-induced change in judgment severity emerges only when cognitive demand is low; high effort conditions abolish the effect or reverse it, indicating conscious processing counteracts automatic priming activation.

Instructional training targeting visual attention and situational awareness improves response speed and hit timing in decision scenarios but does not improve traditional marksmanship, indicating domain-specific rather than general skill transfer.

Auditory flankers produce interference at perceptual (letter recognition) but not response stages, unlike visual flankers which show both effects, indicating cross-modal conflict has modality-specific processing locus.
